_README: Hand-entered usage stats for species the main Reg M-A sets file does not cover
(the new Reg M-B Pokemon and their megas, until Smogon M-B stats land ~July).
Add one top-level key per species (see _schema and _example below); it is merged
into the usage data and feeds every inference: items, abilities, spreads, moves,
- Keys starting with '_' are documentation and are IGNORED by the loader.
- Gap-fill only: a species already present in the main sets file is NOT
overridden. These keys are for species in _todo below.
- Percentages are real usage % (0-100), NOT normalized. You may omit the
'Other' bucket. Dicts need not be sorted; the loader sorts by pct desc.
- Provide only the fields you have; omit the rest.
- A malformed JSON edit fails loud at startup (so a typo can't silently drop

<Species Name>.raw_count: int, optional. Relative popularity. Used ONLY to decide base-vs-mega forme for an unrevealed mon: compared against the '<Name>-Mega' entry's raw_count. Set both when a mon megas (higher = more likely forme). Omit if the mon has no mega.

spreads.<Nature>:HP/Atk/Def/SpA/SpD/Spe: pct. EVs are SP units 0-32 (32 = 252 EVs), same format as the main file and parse_spread(). Drives the speed/Choice-Scarf read.

_notes: For a mega forme, name the entry '<Base>-Mega' (or '-Mega-X' / '-Mega-Y') and
make its mega STONE the highest-% item -- that is what wires mega_stones() and
the stone->forme mapping (so a revealed stone commits to the mega's stats).
Item/ability/move/teammate keys must match the names Showdown uses.

_example._note: Illustrative numbers only -- not real data. Delete this _example block's values pattern by copying it as a real top-level key (without the leading underscore) and replacing with measured usage.

_todo._note: Species with no usage data yet. Move each into a real top-level entry as you compile numbers. Mega-capable bases should also get their '<Base>-Mega' entry.
